# Nedaa | نداء

> Free, open-source, privacy-first prayer-times and Islamic-companion app for iOS and Android. No data collection. No selling. No ads. No accounts. No paywalls. No premium tier.

Nedaa (Arabic: نداء, "the call" — a reference to the Adhan) is built around accurate prayer times, real OS-level Fajr alarms, daily Athkar from the Hisn al-Muslim dataset, Qibla, Hijri tools, an Umrah guide, a Qada (fasting) tracker, and a Quran reader (in active development). It ships on iOS, Google Play (Android with GMS), and Huawei AppGallery (Android with HMS — a deliberately maintained build for Huawei devices without Google Mobile Services).

The app is open source at https://github.com/NedaaDevs/nedaa under [its license](https://github.com/NedaaDevs/nedaa/blob/master/LICENSE). The Tanzil Quran text used in the Quran reader is separately licensed under CC-BY 3.0; attribution is preserved in the app's About screen.

## Pillar feature pages

- [Prayer times](https://nedaa.dev/prayer-times): 22 calculation methods (Muslim World League, ISNA, Umm al-Qura, Karachi, Egyptian, Diyanet, JAKIM, Kemenag, Singapore MUIS, and more); provider-abstracted at the API layer with Aladhan as the currently-implemented upstream; both Hanafi and standard Asr juristic schools; high-latitude adjustment; works offline after first sync.
- [Prayer alarm](https://nedaa.dev/prayer-alarm): real OS-level prayer alarms (Fajr, Jummah, and custom types) — Apple AlarmKit on iOS 26+ (alarm feature requires iOS 26; not available on earlier iOS), native Android foreground alarm service. Optional wake-up challenges (tap or math). Custom Athan sound per alarm type. Bypasses Do Not Disturb.
- [Athkar](https://nedaa.dev/athkar): morning, evening, and occasion-based supplications from the Hisn al-Muslim dataset (Sa'id ibn Ali ibn Wahaf al-Qahtani). Multiple reciter audio voices. Focus-mode reader. Custom user-added athkar. Gentle (non-gamified) habit tracking with pause/resume.
- [Qada fasting tracker](https://nedaa.dev/qada-tracker): counts down missed Ramadan fasts with a Ramadan-anchored reminder for the canonical "make up before next Ramadan" deadline. Privacy-mode hide. For fasting qada only — does not track missed prayers.

## Bilingual (Arabic) versions

- [مواقيت الصلاة](https://nedaa.dev/ar/مواقيت-الصلاة): النسخة العربية لصفحة مواقيت الصلاة.
- [منبه الصلاة](https://nedaa.dev/ar/منبه-الصلاة): النسخة العربية لصفحة منبه صلاة الفجر.
- [الأذكار](https://nedaa.dev/ar/الأذكار): النسخة العربية لصفحة الأذكار.
- [قضاء الصيام](https://nedaa.dev/ar/قضاء-الصيام): النسخة العربية لصفحة متتبع قضاء الصيام.

## Comparison and research

- [How Islamic prayer-time apps differ on privacy, ads, and openness](https://nedaa.dev/comparison/privacy-ads-openness): category-level comparison framework — four dimensions (data collection, advertising, pricing, source transparency) with "questions to ask of any app you're considering." Brand-voice safe; no head-to-head.
- [Why digital Quran apps look the way they do](https://nedaa.dev/research/islamic-typography): long-form piece on Islamic typography for small screens — why Quran apps use page images instead of rendered text, the Uthmani vs Imlaei script question, the QCF page-image editions (v1, v2, v4) from the King Fahd Quran Complex, hybrid image+font rendering, Arabic search across diacritics via SQLite FTS5, and pairing Latin and Arabic typefaces.

## Upstream data sources

- [Aladhan API](https://aladhan.com): currently-implemented prayer-time calculation upstream. All 22 calculation methods Nedaa exposes today come from Aladhan. Nedaa's prayer-time computation is provider-abstracted at the API layer; the architecture supports adding additional providers without app updates.
- [Tanzil Project](https://tanzil.net): Quran text in Uthmani script, CC-BY 3.0. Attribution to "Tanzil Project" preserved in Nedaa's About screen as required by the license.
- [EveryAyah.com](https://everyayah.com): per-ayah Quran reciter audio (planned for the Quran reader's audio layer).
- [Quranic Universal Library (QUL)](https://qul.tarteel.ai) by Tarteel AI: Quran metadata and word-timing reference.
- King Fahd Quran Complex (KFQC), Madinah: page-image editions (QCF v1, v2, v4) of the Madinah Mushaf.
- Hisn al-Muslim by Sa'id ibn Ali ibn Wahaf al-Qahtani: source dataset for athkar.

## Distribution

- iOS App Store: bundle id `dev.nedaa.app`.
- Google Play (Android, GMS): package `dev.nedaa.android`.
- Huawei AppGallery (Android, HMS): same package, separate `production-hms` build with no Google Mobile Services dependencies.
- Source: https://github.com/NedaaDevs/nedaa
- Translation: https://crowdin.com/project/nedaa-v2 (English, Arabic, Malay, Urdu currently shipped; RTL-first design).
- Support: support@nedaa.dev

## Optional

- [Developer guide](https://github.com/NedaaDevs/nedaa/blob/master/docs/DEV-README.md): codebase orientation, build setup (including GMS vs HMS Android variants), native module overview, contribution conventions.
